reviews.review_title: Like Teardown, but SO MUCH BETTER IN VR
reviews.review_description: The destruction physics are so satisfying and fun. The combat is decent. The DEV team really needs to work on the moving mechanics of this because the somehow REALLY messed it up, it is super wobbly and uncontrollable. The only good part of the moving system is that I can stay still easily. I really like the flying mechanic of it but there’s one problem, the way to go up and down is right next to your movement joystick and it is hard to move up and down while flying around (I, basically saying to swap those controls on the right controller). Overall, it is so fun to have this game. You should definitely buy this game if you like seeing buildings crumble to dust amd blow away. The mods are kind of limited so I think the DEV team really needs to host a contest for best mods wins [some high amount of 💰💵💸] so we can get more. (Btw get this game while it’s cheap [witch it REALLY IS] because you never know if the price will go up)

So I just booted it up a second time and confirmed I should refund this when the app just closed on me randomly. 

Three stars just for the potential and idea. The lack of content makes this more like 1-2 stars. 

 I am genuinely so tired of early access games asking for your money with little to no content. I feel like we have more games on the store in early access than actual titles. Please stop releasing them SO early.  In it's current state I don't think this should be for sale. Free- sure.  It needs a lot more work. A lot more content.  Please don't ask people to pay for this. 

I think there's loads of potential here, but it needs something besides survival as a primary mode. In a destruction physics game fighting enemies is fun but I would rather destroy a house than protect it. I hope they add better modes, progression, and a campaign. 

I actually wished teardown was more about the destruction and less about a heist puzzle so please don't go that terrible route either. 

I'll keep track of this game and how much content for the price there seems to be. I do wish them success, but I beg you don't release a game to be purchased this early again, you are a better studio than this. This is just a prime example of a trend I really dislike in VR. 

Also their last game Battle Talent, while the fun factor is there, there is an extreme lack of guidance and after many hours played there's still basic things I have no idea how to do, or even where to find out how. 

I REALLY hope they make the mechanics of this game and player direction A LOT clearer. 

Devs- I could use your help with that game! but they never responded to my review there either.

this game is great it combines wave base zombie shooting with tear downs voxel game play this game is definitely worth the $7 somethings that i think they could add is better physics for grabbing and better optimization sometimes when you're building something you turn on super slow mo and your whole building falls apart but for the price i paid for this game it is 100% worth the download

I bought this game on release and it was perfect until a month ago when I logged in to play I didn’t have a curser. I couldn’t select a game mode to play eventually I figured I have to show me hand menu in order to select stuff and now I can select a map but I can’t actually play the game please let me refund this game it’s just siting in my quest library

developer_response.body:
Hi, please try delete app data and reinstall:
1. Open Settings: Press the Meta button on your right controller to open the universal menu, then select the gear icon (Settings).
2. Navigate to Storage: Select General and then Storage.
3. Find the App: Locate the app or game you want to clear from the list.
4. Delete Data: Click the Delete (trash can) icon next to the app and confirm to wipe its local data

reviews.review_description: The developers really have done great so far on this game and I love that it has mod support although I wish they told you how to make mods. The voxel destruction works as expected and is super satisfying to see! Definitely recommend this game and am excited for future updates. Ignore and reviews talking about hands not working cause they work fine for me.
Great Deva they come out with updates about every month and it is crazy how much potential this game has. Buy it you wont regret it. Devs all we need now is reloading.

I personally think the weapons are too strong. It doesn't really make sense to be able to split a zombie in half with a wrench, and I would love some low caliber guns to really pick apart the enemies piece by piece.

The armor on the zombies head is cool but it feels a little too weak and it still leaves the rest of the body exposed. I think armor in general is a good idea and I would love to see it from the player as well.

I feel like the zombie waves focus too much on quality over quantity, I would really love to see some proper swarms, as well I think the mode would benefit from custom house presets. 

Physical reloading would also be appreciated but an alternative could be making weapons and ammo purchasable separately.

I also think the game could benefit from more zombie variety, like a similar system to the preset house destruction where some zombies will spawn in with parts of their body already missing. 

I think zombies should be a lot more vulnerable to headshots it's a little annoying when they're missing pretty much all of their head and they're still alive, the inside of their skull should also be flesh and not just solid bone.

And finally I think builds are too fragile.

reviews.review_title: Yeah, pretty good. Tweaks are needed.
reviews.review_description: I LOVE this game at its core, but the climbing is horrendous because of how weighted your character is when you climb more Thant 5 feet up. And you can’t vault at all. It’s legitimately impossible to vault. Then, the buildings are super weak. My stone walls, or iron fences are torn down in seconds which is a little sad. Then, they should tweak the exploding zombie so that it’s blast radius is smaller. Also, guns are a little weird to hold, since you can’t hold them  with both hands. But besides all that, very fun and interesting game. Seriously, all I did was criticize it. But don’t think that this game isn’t amazing. It has good potential, and after playing for about 10 hours, I can confirm it’s worth the whopping seven bucks.

reviews.review_title: A Masterpiece Mix of Sandbox and Surviva
reviews.review_description:
Voxel Playground is an incredible blend of Minecraft’s creativity and the thrill of survival mechanics. As a sandbox lover, I’m already excited about the potential for future mods! The game has so much potential.

However, the most urgent update needed is localization. Even if full translation takes time, the basic tutorials must support multiple languages. Understanding the core controls is essential for new players to fully enjoy everything Voxel Playground has to offer. I really hope to see Korean and other languages added in the upcoming updates.

Keep up the great work!

reviews.review_title: Fun but not worth 7 dollars, maybe 5
reviews.review_description: It's really gun and semi polished, just REALLY needs more content. It's not exactly what I expected from a game with teardown like physics, but if you added multi-player for shenanigans, of more enemy types (I see the enemy type option in setup) it would be awesome. It has so much potential, especially coming from the creators of Battle Talent (great game). Don't buy if you want massive destruction like skyscrapers, buy if you want a genuinely gun experience to just go in and brutalize zombies.

reviews.review_description:
Like the title says, I think this game has gone under the radar. It has A LOT of potential. It feels like Bonelab, but with voxels, and the physics are great. The weapons, enemies, and combat feel really satisfying, even the soundtrack is great!

The main things that I would say are missing are more maps, bigger environments (I wish there was like a city, at least on the Steam version because I know standalone might not handle it), and some kind of campaign or structured progression. It often feels like it’s just zombies constantly knowing where you are and always following you. It would be better to have enemies feel more reactive and less omniscient. Maybe with line of sight or detection systems so you can sneak around.

This might be a stretch, but I also think it would be great if there were resources to find or any reason to explore, and a survival mode with more purpose. It has a bit of that Death Road to Canada vibe already, and I think leaning into that could make it amazing. Like instead of focusing just on surviving the waves of constant zombies, to have to survive for x days even without having to kill zombies, just like actual apocalyptic survival, that would turn this game into a Masterpiece in my opinion.

So yeah, I just wish it felt like more than a sandbox. Even the survival still feels a little too “sandboxy.”

All in all, I really think this game deserves a lot more attention. I don’t know how I hadn’t heard of it until now. I saw the trailer and was instantly hooked, and the game didn’t disappoint at all! It just made me want even more from it, I guess.

reviews.review_description: This is one of the best games I played in a while please keep updating this game

There’s a lot of potential in this game, and I LOVE Voxel games. But I feel like there’s so much untapped potential in this game. For one, whenever you run out of ammo for a gun, you can’t reload it or anything. It just breaks. I hate that. And the melee weapons have durability which for this type of game, I don’t agree on since it’s a wave/horde based game right now. Fix these things, and then focus on getting better, bigger maps put in. Ones where it’s not just one simple house surrounded by open space. It’s kinda boring. Anyways, I love everything else about this game and hope that it’ll grow into something great!
